title: task
description: framing.rs:391-398 - When CMD byte equals FEND (0xC0, port 12), the inter-frame padding skip loop incorrectly treats CMD as another FEND delimiter. For frame [FEND, 0xC0, 0x41, FEND] (port 12, cmd 0, data 'A'), the reader parses as port=4, cmd=1, data=[] instead. Affects all port 12 frames. kiss_decode() handles this correctly by only skipping trailing FENDs; KissReader needs similar fix to distinguish opening FEND + CMD from inter-frame FEND padding.
close_reason: Already fixed: FEND-skipping loop stops when <3 bytes remain (handles CMD=0xC0), and closing FEND search starts after CMD byte
